blocks.title: Pronoun Cases and Comparisons
blocks.explanation: Subject pronouns must be used after comparative connectors like 'than' or 'as' when comparing subjects.

concepts.definition: Always use subject case (he/she/I) after 'than' or 'as' in subjective comparison.

examples.rule: Use the nominative/subjective case after than/as.
examples.working: He is taller than I (am taller).
